response:
I read `ISSUE-109.md` and cross-checked it against ADR 0035 (the closing verdict on issue #41's tiered-execution experiment) and the relevant pre-registration precedents (ADR 0019, 0023, 0024, 0025, 0027). Decision recorded as `docs/decisions/0036-routing-cascade-experiment-greenlight.md`.

**Decision: PROCEED, conditionally.**

Key reasoning:
- ADR 0035 rejected *tiered* (iterative coaching of a weak implementer) — the identified failure mechanism was "iterating a weak implementer against a strong critic converges to the implementer's ceiling." Routing (single haiku attempt, discard-and-redo on reject, no coaching) is a genuinely different mechanism that was never tested, so this isn't re-litigating a settled ADR — it's a first test of an unexamined shape.
- The issue's own cost model bounds the downside well below tiered's blowup: worst case ≈ checker + sonnet-solo cost, not tiered's 2.94x.
- I found a way to make this even cheaper than the issue proposed: routing's escalate branch (sonnet redoes the whole task, no coaching) is *identical* to the already-collected `sonnet-solo` arm in `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/`, and that benchmark's `haiku-solo` outputs are already graded too. So Phase 1 — the checker-fidelity pilot that determines whether routing can even work — needs **zero new generation spend**, only checker-call tokens, by composing already-graded cells.
- Gate: proceed to a full routing grid / adopt-reject verdict only if a checker variant clears **≤15% false-accept** (the issue's own threshold, benchmarked against ADR 0035's measured 25% tiered-validator false-accept). If nothing clears it, record the null immediately rather than spending on a full grid to chase a result the pilot already ruled out.

One process note: I couldn't get `adr-lint.mjs` to actually execute in this session (repeated approval prompts weren't granted), so I hand-verified the frontmatter, ADR citations, char budget (5909/6000), and the falsifiability criterion against the linter's source. Please run `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions` yourself before merging to get the automated confirmation CLAUDE.md requires pre-merge.

The Phase 1 pilot script itself is follow-up work, not done here — per ADR 0021 it belongs in a GitHub issue (deferred, not implemented inline in this decision).
